title: pi0.7: a Steerable Generalist Robotic Foundation Model with Emergent Capabilities
why: Physical Intelligence's 2026 generalist robotic foundation model; important for compositional generalization, multimodal prompting, and steering at inference time.

title: GEN-1: Scaling Embodied Foundation Models to Mastery
category: commercial physical AI / no open paper
why: Generalist AI's 2026 model claims production-level reliability, speed, and improvisation; no public arXiv paper found, so track architecture claims and evidence quality.

title: GENE-26.5: Genesis AI Robotic Brain for Human-Level Manipulation
why: Genesis AI's 2026 full-stack robotics system emphasizes human-scale dexterous hand, tactile glove data, simulation, and a data engine; no public arXiv paper found.

title: Gemini Robotics: Bringing AI into the Physical World
why: Important Google DeepMind VLA system connecting Gemini-style multimodal reasoning to robot action.

title: GR00T N1: An Open Foundation Model for Generalist Humanoid Robots
why: Open humanoid foundation model with dual-system VLA architecture and synthetic/real/human-video training mixture.

title: OneTwoVLA: A Unified Vision-Language-Action Model with Adaptive Reasoning
why: Unifies System 1 acting and System 2 reasoning with adaptive switching during execution.

title: UniVLA: Learning to Act Anywhere with Task-centric Latent Actions
why: Uses latent actions from broad video data for cross-embodiment policy learning.

title: Unified Vision-Language-Action Model
why: Models vision, language, and action as token sequences and uses world-model post-training.

title: TriVLA: A Triple-System-Based Unified Vision-Language-Action Model for General Robot Control
why: Adds episodic world modeling and a triple-system architecture for dynamic embodied tasks.

title: DreamVLA: A Vision-Language-Action Model Dreamed with Comprehensive World Knowledge
why: Uses predicted world knowledge rather than raw image forecasting to improve action prediction.

title: F1: A Vision-Language-Action Model Bridging Understanding and Generation to Actions
why: Bridges understanding, foresight generation, and action via future visual state prediction.

title: FLOWER: Democratizing Generalist Robot Policies with Efficient Vision-Language-Action Flow Policies
why: Sub-billion-parameter flow VLA focused on compute-efficient open generalist robot policies.

title: LLaDA-VLA: Vision Language Diffusion Action Models
why: Explores masked diffusion VLMs for VLA policy learning and hierarchical action decoding.

title: SimpleVLA-RL: Scaling VLA Training via Reinforcement Learning
why: Applies scalable RL to VLA training to improve long-horizon planning and distribution-shift robustness.

title: MoS-VLA: A Vision-Language-Action Model with One-Shot Skill Adaptation
why: Introduces mixture-of-skills adaptation for fast domain/task adaptation from one demonstration.
